note: Common public confusions and source tensions, with the resolution the script must follow. v002: CON-04 (Markopolos warnings) is now resolved against OIG-509.

contradictions.topic: Size of the fraud: $65B vs $17.5B vs $170B
positions: $65 billion: total fabricated value across customer statements (paper, includes fictional profits).
~$17.5 billion: actual lost principal (cash in, per the trustee).
$170 billion: a forfeiture/asset figure cited at sentencing (funds that flowed through accounts), not net investor loss.
contradictions.resolution: Treat the three numbers as different measures. State plainly: $65B = fake account value, ~$17.5B = real principal lost, and avoid using $170B as 'the amount stolen'. This distinction is a deliberate myth-correction beat (CLM-0004).

positions: Popular shorthand: 'Madoff's company was a fraud.'
Reality: the broker-dealer/market-making business was a real operation; the investment ADVISORY arm was the Ponzi.
contradictions.resolution: Specify that the fraud was the advisory arm; do not claim the entire firm was fictional (CLM-0003).

positions: Headline impression: investors were wiped out.
Reality: the trustee/DOJ clawed back most principal; many recovered >90%.
contradictions.resolution: Acknowledge devastating initial losses AND the large eventual recovery; do not say everyone lost everything or that everyone was made whole (CLM-0009).

contradictions.topic: How many times / which years did Markopolos warn the SEC?
positions: OIG-509 frames it at the agency level: the SEC received six substantive complaints between June 1992 and December 2008.
contradictions.resolution: RESOLVED against OIG-509. Narration may keep 'for roughly a decade, repeatedly'; for any exact statement use Markopolos submissions in 2000, 2001, 2005, 2007, 2008, and the agency-level 'six substantive complaints, 1992-2008'. The 2005 memo title is 'The World's Largest Hedge Fund Is a Fraud' (2005-11-07). (CLM-0005)

positions: He PLEADED guilty; there was no trial.
contradictions.resolution: Say 'pleaded guilty'; never 'a jury found him guilty' (CLM-0001).
